veal-nois-casserole-argiro.name: Veal Nois Casserole

ingredients.item: 1 pieces of single mind about 1,500 gr.
1 pieces of single mind about 1,500 gr.

instructions: Rinse the mushrooms first and add them to a bowl.
Cover with hot water and let them soak for 10-15′′.
Drain the mushrooms and keep the water.
Strain it in a coffee filter.
Tap the mind with absorbent paper to dry.
Tie the meat with baking string to keep its shape during cooking.
Leave for 40′ to come to room temperature.
We rub pepper in the calf mind.
Heat the olive oil in a strong heat and add the meat around to make a crust and pink color.
We put it on a platter and keep it warm.
Lower the heat and add onion, celery, carrot, garlic, dried mushrooms and herbs.
In low heat stirring regularly let the vegetables soften and caramelize.
Add the wine and let it evaporate completely.
Add the homemade beef broth and hot water and mix the ingredients.
Put the meat back in and when it comes to boiling, lower the heat, add foil and lid and simmer the meat until it almost melts. For about two hours.
While cooking the beef noodles in the pot, turn it into its sauce.
Cut the meat into pieces and serve with potato mashed potatoes and vegetable soup.

vegan-chocolate-blended-baked-oats.name: Vegan Chocolate Blended Baked Oats
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Spray two 8-ounce ramekins with cooking spray on the bottom and sides.

Combine oats, almond milk, banana, cocoa powder, maple syrup, baking powder, vanilla extract, and salt in a blender. Blend until all is combined well, 30 to 60 seconds. Fill mixture into the prepared ramekins and sprinkle each with 1 tablespoon chocolate chips.
Bake in the preheated oven until firm, about 25 minutes.

vegan-mushroom-risotto.name: Vegan Mushroom Risotto
instructions: Pour vegetable stock into a large pot and bring to a boil over medium heat. Reduce heat to low, cover, and keep warm.

Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 10 minutes. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in celery and garlic, and cook until garlic is fragrant, about 3 minutes.

Add remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil and Arborio rice to the Dutch oven. Cook, stirring often, until rice is opaque and smells slightly toasted, 2 to 4 minutes. Stir in white wine; cook until it evaporates, an additional 2 to 3 minutes. Season with thyme, salt, and pepper.

Begin stirring in reserved warm vegetable stock, one cup at a time; allow each addition of stock to be fully absorbed before adding in the next, stirring continuously. Continue adding broth gradually, stirring constantly, until rice is tender and creamy, about 20 minutes. Season with additional salt and pepper.

vegan-rhubarb-bran-muffins.name: Vegan Rhubarb Bran Muffins
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners.
Combine flour, rhubarb, brown sugar, wheat bran, baking powder, cinnamon, and nutmeg in a large bowl.
Combine applesauce, oil, molasses, vinegar, and vanilla extract in a separate bowl; slowly mix into flour mixture until a thick batter forms. Divide batter among the prepared muffin cups.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into centers comes out clean, 15 to 20 minutes.

venison-tenderloin-in-the-slow-cooker.name: Venison Tenderloin In The Slow Cooker

ingredients.item: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Place mushrooms in a small heat-proof bowl; cover with boiling water and soak until soft, about 15 minutes. Drain; reserve soaking liquid. Finely chop mushrooms; add to reserved soaking liquid and set aside.
Rinse venison under cold running water; pat thoroughly dry with paper towels. Cut venison into ¾-inch slices.

Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear venison slices, in batches, until lightly browned on both sides, making sure not to overcrowd the skillet, 5 to 10 minutes per batch. Transfer venison to a slow cooker.

Add mushrooms, soaking liquid, tomatoes, shallots, red wine, balsamic vinegar, vegetable broth powder, garlic, bay leaves, herbes de Provence, juniper berries, thyme, sage, and herb salt to slow cooker; stir well.
Cover slow cooker. Cook on Low for 3 hours.
Whisk 3 tablespoons cooking liquid and cornstarch together in a small bowl until cornstarch is dissolved; stir into slow cooker until well combined.
Cook until sauce has thickened and venison is tender, about 1 hour more, stirring occasionally; season with salt and black pepper.

verde-chilaquiles-with-eggs.name: Verde Chilaquiles With Eggs
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and jalapeno; cook, stirring occasionally, until onion begins to brown, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a very large bowl. Add salsa, chicken, chips, olives, and cilantro. Toss to coat, breaking chips slightly. Transfer to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Use the bottom of a custard or measuring cup to make 8 indentations in the chip mixture. Crack 1 egg into each indentation. Sprinkle eggs evenly with salt and black pepper.
Bake until eggs are set and tortilla chips are softened and browned at edges, 20 to 25 minutes. Sprinkle with cotija cheese, radishes, and avocado.
